summary: Mirror parity cannot be byte-for-byte because Gemini strips unsupported OpenCode frontmatter and Codex wraps the body in TOML developer_instructions.
deep-research-contract-parity.vitest.ts provides the closest reusable shape: enumerate runtime mirrors and assert contract markers across each file.
The deep-research parity test is stale as a template because it references .opencode/skills/sk-deep-research while the live folder is .opencode/skills/deep-research.
A general agent mirror contract checker would serve multi-ai-council first while preserving ADR-001, because enforcement stays in system-spec-kit tests instead of creating a dedicated council skill.
Raw SHA/hash comparison is ruled out because runtime wrappers differ by design; use normalized body extraction plus required marker assertions.
